title: euphrasia insignis var. togakusiensis
intro: Euphrasia insignis var. togakusiensis, also known as Togakushi gentian or Japanese gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its unique white or pink flowers and native to the Togakushi mountains in Japan. The medicinal properties of Euphrasia insignis var. togakusiensis include its rich content of iridoids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to improve vision, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, as well as its traditional use in treating various eye disorders. In herbal preparations, Euphrasia insignis var. togakusiensis is often used in eye drops, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
